witnesses.description: One exact Wolfe line-search witness for f(x)=x^2 from x=1 along direction -2.

checks.claim: At x=1 for f(x)=x^2, direction -2 is a descent direction with directional derivative -4.
checks.notes: The validator differentiates the quadratic and recomputes gradient * direction.

checks.claim: The exact line minimizer along direction -2 is alpha=1/2 and reaches x=0.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes x+alpha*d, f(candidate), the gradient, and the zero directional derivative at the minimizer.

checks.claim: The accepted step alpha=1/2 satisfies Wolfe sufficient decrease with slack 1/2.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the Armijo right-hand side f(x)+c1*alpha*phi'(0) and the nonnegative slack.

checks.claim: The accepted step alpha=1/2 satisfies the Wolfe curvature bound with slack 2.
checks.notes: The validator checks |phi'(alpha)| <= c2*|phi'(0)| over exact rationals.

checks.claim: The full step alpha=1 satisfies the same Wolfe curvature bound.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact replay computes curvature violation 2 for alpha=1, so the malformed nonpositive-violation claim is rejected by checked Farkas evidence.

checks.claim: General Wolfe line-search existence, convergence, and rate theorems require future Lean reconstruction.

data.target_theorem_shape: Wolfe and strong-Wolfe line-search existence, descent lemmas, Zoutendijk-style convergence theorems, and rate statements for smooth optimization methods
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies plus exact rational certificate bridges for finite Wolfe line-search witnesses

checks.notes: The finite rows check only one rational quadratic Wolfe instance; Wolfe line-search theory remains proof-assistant work.
